name: idkwidk-template-provisioner
description: Guided setup for the idkWidk SaaS template across GitHub, Vercel, Convex, Clerk, and Codex without leaking organization credentials into the repo.

interface.shortDescription: Walks a user or agent through secure SaaS template setup.
interface.longDescription: idkWidk Template Provisioner is a local setup guide for this template. It stores reusable provider access outside the repo, runs read-only checks before live provider creation, explains every step in plain language, and keeps project-specific runtime values in ignored env files or provider env stores.
defaultPrompt: Use idkWidk Template Provisioner as the setup control center. Run npm run setup:agent -- --project <project-slug>, use the JSON form when automation needs a parseable next action, explain whether user input is required, and do not run live provider mutations without explicit user approval.

Use idkWidk Template Provisioner as the new-project factory. Run npm run setup:factory -- --project <project-slug>, then npm run setup:completion -- --project <project-slug>, explain each step in plain English, store reusable provider credentials only in the local vault/keychain, and do not run live provider mutations without explicit user approval.

Use idkWidk Template Provisioner. Run npm run setup:bootstrap -- --project <project-slug> first for the lowest-lift guided path, explain the result in plain English, and do not run live provider mutations without explicit user approval.

Use idkWidk Template Provisioner to set up this SaaS template on a new computer. Run npm run setup:onboard -- --project <project-slug>, keep organization tokens in the local vault, keep project env files ignored, and verify provider readbacks before claiming setup is done.
